demographics.living_situation: newborn at home with both parents

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: It's our 5-day-old baby boy. He's completely stopped waking up for his feeds, his legs are stiffly pedalling in the air like he's on a bike, and his wee smells really strongly like maple syrup. Please help.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: He's been getting worse all day — over the last 12 hours he's gone from just sleepy and off his feeds to barely responding, and now these strange stiff movements have started.

symptom_narrative.onset: he's been a poor feeder and bringing up milk since the first days of life, but the sleepiness and strange movements have come on fast over the last 12 hours
symptom_narrative.duration: worsening over about 12 hours, on a background of a week of poor feeding
symptom_narrative.character: started as being hard to wake and not interested in feeds; now he's floppy one moment and stiff the next, with his legs pedalling and his body going into a rigid stretched-out posture; his wee and even his earwax smell sweet, like maple syrup or burnt sugar
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: we're terrified — he's hardly responding to us now and his breathing looks fast and heavy
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: his whole body — the movements are mostly his legs, and the smell is in his nappy and around his ears
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and getting worse, not coming and going
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps getting worse on its own no matter what we do
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: vomiting, not waking for feeds, floppy then stiff, fast breathing, the sweet smell
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: none — he seemed okay at birth, this is the first time
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he won't feed at all now and is barely rousable

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: born at term by a normal delivery, seemed healthy at birth with good Apgar scores

family_history_as_reported.narrative: no known family history of any similar problem; parents are otherwise well

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: first baby, exclusively formula-fed on a standard infant formula since birth, both parents at home and present on the call

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: newborn; poor feeding and failure to regain birth weight since birth, now with rapid neurological deterioration reported by parents

objective_data_offered.value: baby is very floppy and hard to rouse, then stiffens with his legs pedalling in the air and his body stretched out rigidly; breathing looks fast and deep
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: parent-held video during a distressing emergency; movements and breathing effort are hard to judge remotely

objective_data_offered.value: his wee and his earwax smell strongly sweet, like maple syrup or burnt sugar
he has been vomiting and refusing feeds, and hasn't put on weight — he actually seems lighter than when we brought him home
standard high-protein infant formula in the kitchen — it's all he has been fed since birth
